close_reason: Cannot fix: bitwarden SDK's login_access_token takes String, not Zeroizing<String>. Upstream SDK change required.
description: src/lib.rs:150 calls access_token.to_string() to satisfy AccessTokenLoginRequest::access_token: String. The original is Zeroizing<String>, but the SDK's copy is a plain String inside the request struct, then potentially retained inside the SDK's internal session state (the SDK's login_method stores the access token id and client secret components after login). The crate's docstring (lib.rs:28-33) calls out that SecretResponse::value is not zeroized, but does NOT call out that the access TOKEN itself is also leaked into the SDK's non-zeroized memory. This is a defense-in-depth gap: an attacker with a memory dump can extract the access token from the SDK's non-zeroized buffers even after BitwardenBackend is dropped. Fixes (in order of preference): (1) document the limitation in the existing zeroization section of the lib.rs docstring (cheap), (2) drop the access_token field from BitwardenBackend after first successful login since the OnceCell already holds an authenticated client, so the long-lived copy in self.access_token is redundant once session is initialized — though refresh() requires it if we ever support session expiry, (3) upstream feature request to bitwarden-sdk to use Zeroizing<String> for credential fields.
title: Access token cloned into bitwarden SDK as plain non-zeroizing String
